title: Maintain a catalog of supported jurisdictions
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to maintain a catalog of supported jurisdictions such as countries, states, cities, or other operational regions, so that the platform has a governed, reusable set of regions relevant for operations, taxation, and policy applicability.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the jurisdictions catalog, when I add a new jurisdiction, then it is stored as a catalog entry available for use across the platform.; Given the catalog exists, when I view it, then I can see all supported jurisdiction entries relevant for operations, taxation, and policy applicability.; Given a jurisdiction entry, when I edit or update it, then the changes are reflected wherever the jurisdiction is referenced.; Given a jurisdiction entry, when I attempt to add a duplicate, then the system prevents duplicate catalog entries.; Given a jurisdiction is referenced by companies, tax/fee settings, or policies, when I attempt to delete or deactivate it, then the system warns of or prevents removal to protect existing references.

title: Support multiple jurisdiction types as catalog entries
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to record jurisdictions of different types — countries, states, cities, or other operational regions — as catalog entries, so that the catalog can represent whatever operational region granularity is needed.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a jurisdiction, when I choose its type, then I can select country, state, city, or other operational region.; Given jurisdictions of different types exist, when I browse the catalog, then each entry's type is identifiable.; Given the catalog, when I need a non-standard region, then I can record it as an "other operational region" type without forcing it into a country/state/city classification.
source: FR 6.4.1 (countries, states, cities, or other operational regions)

title: Manage jurisdictions as flat catalog entries, not a mandatory hierarchy
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want jurisdictions to be managed as independent catalog entries rather than a mandatory geographic hierarchy, so that I can add regions flexibly without being forced to model parent-child geographic relationships.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create a jurisdiction entry, when I save it, then I am not required to attach it under a parent geographic node.; Given the catalog, when I add a city or region, then it can exist independently without a mandated country/state chain.; Given a jurisdiction entry, when I manage it, then it is treated as a catalog entry rather than a node in a mandatory geographic hierarchy.
source: FR 6.4.1 (catalog entries rather than mandatory geographic hierarchy)

title: Define tax and fee applicability on a jurisdiction
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to define tax and fee applicability on a jurisdiction where configured, so that operations tied to that jurisdiction can reflect the correct taxes and fees.
acceptance_criteria: Given a jurisdiction entry, when I configure its tax and fee applicability, then those settings are saved against the jurisdiction.; Given a jurisdiction with tax and fee applicability configured, when it is referenced in operations, then the configured tax and fee applicability is available for use.; Given a jurisdiction without tax and fee configuration, when it is used, then the system treats tax and fee applicability as not configured (optional, only where configured).; Given tax and fee applicability is updated on a jurisdiction, when it is referenced downstream, then the updated applicability is reflected.
source: FR 6.4.2 (tax and fee applicability where configured)

title: Use jurisdictions as policy applicability criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to use jurisdictions as applicability criteria when defining policies, so that policies apply correctly based on the jurisdiction they are relevant to.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring a policy, when I set applicability criteria, then I can select one or more jurisdictions from the catalog.; Given a policy scoped to a jurisdiction, when the policy is evaluated, then it applies according to the selected jurisdiction criteria.; Given a policy uses jurisdiction criteria, when the jurisdiction is updated in the catalog, then the policy applicability reflects the current catalog entry.; Given an employee or operation falls outside a policy's jurisdiction criteria, when the policy is evaluated, then it does not apply.

title: Assign one or more jurisdictions to a company
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want my company to operate in one or more jurisdictions, so that the company's operations, taxation, and policies can span every region in which it is active.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I assign jurisdictions to it, then I can add one or more jurisdictions from the catalog.; Given a company operating in multiple jurisdictions, when I view its configuration, then all associated jurisdictions are listed.; Given a company, when I remove a jurisdiction association, then the company no longer operates in that jurisdiction.; Given a company, when I assign jurisdictions, then only supported catalog entries can be selected.
source: FR 6.4.3 (a company may operate in one or more jurisdictions)

title: Reference catalog jurisdictions consistently across modules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want jurisdiction assignments and policy/tax references to draw from the single supported catalog, so that taxation and policy applicability stay consistent across the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given the jurisdiction catalog, when I assign a jurisdiction to a company or policy, then I can only select from supported catalog entries.; Given a jurisdiction is updated in the catalog, when it is referenced by a company, tax/fee setting, or policy, then the reference reflects the current catalog entry.; Given multiple modules reference jurisdictions, when they resolve a jurisdiction, then they all draw from the same single supported catalog.
source: FR 6.4.1–6.4.3 (single supported catalog underpinning company, tax, and policy usage)

title: Oversee jurisdiction usage across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to view which jurisdictions the companies across my portfolio operate in, so that I can oversee cross-company operational, tax, and policy coverage from the supported catalog.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies across my portfolio, when I review jurisdiction usage, then I can see which supported catalog jurisdictions each company operates in.; Given a company operates in one or more jurisdictions, when I inspect the portfolio, then all associated jurisdictions are visible for oversight.; Given jurisdiction assignments draw from the single catalog, when I review them, then the same governed catalog entries are shown consistently.
source: FR 6.4.1, FR 6.4.3 (portfolio oversight of catalog-driven company jurisdictions)

title: Assign jurisdictions across companies in a group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to assign and review the jurisdictions in which the companies of my group operate, so that group-wide operations, taxation, and policy applicability are aligned to the supported catalog.
acceptance_criteria: Given the companies within my group, when I assign jurisdictions, then I can add one or more supported catalog jurisdictions per company.; Given companies in my group operate in multiple jurisdictions, when I view group configuration, then all associated jurisdictions are listed per company.; Given a jurisdiction is removed from a company in the group, when I review it, then that company no longer operates in that jurisdiction.; Given assignments draw from the single supported catalog, when I select jurisdictions, then only catalog entries are available.
source: FR 6.4.1, FR 6.4.3 (group-level assignment of company jurisdictions from the catalog)

title: See jurisdiction-driven policies and taxes applied to me
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the policies and tax/fee treatment that apply to me to reflect the jurisdiction(s) I operate under, so that my policy applicability and taxation are correct for my region.
acceptance_criteria: Given I operate under a company jurisdiction, when policies with jurisdiction applicability criteria are evaluated, then only the policies relevant to my jurisdiction apply to me.; Given my jurisdiction has tax and fee applicability configured, when relevant operations are processed, then the configured tax and fee applicability is reflected for me.; Given my jurisdiction has no tax and fee configuration, when operations are processed, then tax and fee applicability is treated as not configured.
source: FR 6.4.2 (jurisdiction-driven policy and tax applicability for employees)

title: Ensure a company operates in at least one jurisdiction
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each company to be associated with at least one jurisdiction from the catalog, so that its operations, taxation, and policy applicability always resolve to a valid supported region.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I configure it, then I can associate one or more jurisdictions and cannot reduce it below the required minimum of one where the company operates.; Given a company operating in multiple jurisdictions, when I manage associations, then adding or removing jurisdictions keeps at least one active association where required.; Given only supported catalog entries exist, when I select company jurisdictions, then invalid or non-catalog jurisdictions cannot be assigned.
source: FR 6.4.3 (a company operates in one or more jurisdictions)

title: Persist jurisdictions as effective-dated catalog records with history
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want each jurisdiction catalog entry and its tax/fee applicability to be stored as effective-dated records with full change history, so that operations, taxation, and policy applicability resolve correctly as of any point in time and past decisions remain auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create or update a jurisdiction entry, when I save it, then the record is stored with effective-dated validity and its prior state is preserved as history rather than overwritten.; Given a jurisdiction has changed over time, when a company operation or policy is evaluated as of a given date, then the jurisdiction and its tax/fee applicability effective on that date are used.; Given the catalog, when I inspect a jurisdiction, then I can view the history of changes and their effective periods.; Given a jurisdiction is referenced, when I query it, then uniqueness and referential integrity constraints protect against duplicate or orphaned catalog entries.
source: FR 6.4.1 (L1 domain/data: catalog entries as effective-dated records with history & integrity)

title: Govern jurisdiction tax/fee and policy rule-packs as versioned effective-dated config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want jurisdiction tax/fee applicability and policy-applicability criteria to be defined as versioned, effective-dated rule-packs that the engines read, so that regional rules can be changed and rolled forward without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a jurisdiction, when I configure its tax/fee applicability and policy-applicability rules, then they are captured as governed, versioned config the engines consume rather than hard-coded logic.; Given a rule-pack, when I publish a new version with an effective date, then prior versions are retained and the correct version is applied by effective date.; Given I change a jurisdiction's rules, when engines next evaluate applicability, then the updated config is honored without any code deployment.; Given per-tenant configuration, when I govern jurisdiction rules, then changes are scoped and versioned so they can be audited and reverted.
source: FR 6.4.2 (L2 config/metadata: versioned effective-dated jurisdiction rule-packs)

title: Have the Rules engine evaluate jurisdiction as applicability criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared Rules engine to evaluate jurisdiction as a decision-table input when determining tax/fee and policy applicability, so that the correct outcome is produced consistently wherever jurisdiction drives a decision.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy or tax/fee rule uses jurisdiction as a criterion, when the Rules engine evaluates a company operation or employee context, then it reads the jurisdiction config and returns the matching applicability outcome.; Given an operation's jurisdiction does not match any rule condition, when the engine evaluates it, then no jurisdiction-driven applicability is applied.; Given multiple jurisdictions apply to a company, when the engine evaluates applicability, then each relevant jurisdiction's rules are resolved deterministically from the same catalog and config.; Given the jurisdiction config changes, when the engine next evaluates, then it interprets the current effective config without code changes.
source: FR 6.4.2 (L3 engines: Rules engine evaluating jurisdiction decision criteria)

title: Manage the jurisdiction catalog through a metadata-driven screen
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a metadata-driven catalog screen with search, filtering, and navigation for jurisdictions, so that I can efficiently find, add, and maintain supported jurisdictions and their tax/fee settings.
acceptance_criteria: Given the jurisdictions catalog screen, when it renders, then it presents the catalog as a searchable, filterable grid driven by the jurisdiction metadata.; Given many jurisdiction entries exist, when I search or filter by type or name, then only matching entries are shown.; Given I select a jurisdiction, when I open it, then I can view and edit its attributes and tax/fee applicability through metadata-driven forms.; Given I add or update an entry via the screen, when I save, then the change is validated against catalog constraints and reflected across referencing modules.
source: FR 6.4.1, FR 6.4.2 (L4 presentation: metadata-driven catalog screen with search/navigation)
